dashboard_ui.benchmarking_hint: Upload CSVs for other hotels in the same chain to enable cross-hotel benchmarking.

onboarding.page_subtitle: Import incident or job-order data. Select a file, choose an upload mode, and submit.
onboarding.dropzone_aria: Upload CSV file — drag and drop or click to browse

upload_mode.replace_desc: Overwrite existing records for this dataset scope.
upload_mode.append_desc: Add new records without removing previous records.
upload_mode.upsert_desc: Insert new records and update matching existing records.

kpi_notes_im.kpi_01: Total number of incidents logged in the selected period.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_02: Percentage of incidents resolved (Completed status) out of all incidents.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_03: Percentage of incidents still open or pending — not yet completed or cancelled.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_04: Count of incidents currently open or in a pending status.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_05: Count of incidents that were withdrawn or cancelled.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_06: Share of incidents tagged with VIP code.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_08: Share of incidents belonging to a repeated room + category + item combination.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_09: Average minutes between incident creation and first investigation update.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_10: Weighted average severity. Low=1, Medium=2, High=3, Critical=4.

hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_01: Total incident case count in selected scope. Good: lower with stable service quality; Bad: sustained increase > 15% period-over-period without demand growth context.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_02: Operational workload baseline for staffing and queue planning. Good: predictable volume with steady closure quality; Bad: volatility spikes that exceed planned capacity.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_03: Portion of incidents resolved within SLA discipline rules. Good >= 95%; Watch 90-94.9%; Bad < 90%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_04: Measures clean closure quality against active queue. Good >= 95%; Watch 90-94.9%; Bad < 90%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_05: Effectiveness of returning service to normal state after incidents. Good >= 95%; Watch 90-94.9%; Bad < 90%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_06: Standard closure throughput KPI for execution health. Good >= 95%; Watch 90-94.9%; Bad < 90%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_07: Share of critical-severity incidents indicating severe failure exposure. Good <= 1%; Watch 1-2%; Bad > 2%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_08: Average severity intensity of all incident cases. Good <= 1.8; Watch 1.81-2.4; Bad > 2.4.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_09: Resolution quality for VIP-impact incidents. Good >= 95%; Watch 90-94.9%; Bad < 90%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_10: Premium guest incident exposure for brand-protection monitoring. Good <= 6%; Watch 6-10%; Bad > 10%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_11: Legacy-compatible VIP risk share for trend continuity across dashboard versions. Good <= 6%; Watch 6-10%; Bad > 10%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_12: Concentration in top department; high concentration implies bottleneck risk. Good <= 30%; Watch 30-45%; Bad > 45%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_13: Recurrence hotspot indicator tied to unresolved root causes. Good <= 15%; Watch 15-25%; Bad > 25%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_14: Repeat load share for longitudinal comparison with historical reporting baselines. Good <= 15%; Watch 15-25%; Bad > 25%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_15: Top complaint-source concentration to prioritize channel-level fixes. Good <= 35%; Watch 35-50%; Bad > 50%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_16: Open workload pressure currently unresolved. Good <= 5%; Watch 5-10%; Bad > 10%.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_17: Current unresolved queue size requiring active follow-up. Good: stable near 0; Bad: sustained growth over multiple days/weeks.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_18: Peak hourly load marker for shift planning and staffing. Good: balanced hourly pattern; Bad: sharp single-hour spikes without staffing alignment.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_19: Average response latency from case creation to first investigation update. Good <= 30 min; Watch 31-60 min; Bad > 60 min.
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_20: Volume of cancelled cases to monitor process leakage. Good <= 2%; Watch 2-5%; Bad > 5% of total incidents.

hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_03: (Total Cases - SLA Breach Cases) / Total Cases * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_08: Severity Score Sum / Total Cases (Low=1, Medium=2, High=3, Critical=4)
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_15: Top Complaint Source Cases / Total Cases * 100

kpi_notes_jo.kpi_01: Total volume of job orders in scope.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_04: Percentage of jobs ending in timeout.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_05: Share of jobs escalated for intervention.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_06: Share of jobs reassigned across teams.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_07: Average minutes from create to acknowledge.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_09: Average minutes from create to completion.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_10: Total requested quantity across all jobs.

corp_kpi_notes.kpi_01: Composite corporate health index balancing severity, VIP exposure, and SLA breach risk. Benchmark: Good >= 85, Watch 70-84.9, Bad < 70.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_02: Share of incidents classified as Critical. Benchmark: Good <= 1%, Watch 1-2%, Bad > 2%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_03: Average cross-hotel benchmark index for fair chain-level comparison. Benchmark: Good >= 85, Watch 75-84.9, Bad < 75.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_04: Portion of incidents involving VIP guests; tracks premium-service risk. Benchmark: Good <= 6%, Watch 6-10%, Bad > 10%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_05: Operational discipline KPI based on breach/late/overdue-like statuses. Benchmark: Good <= 3%, Watch 3-5%, Bad > 5%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_06: Percentage of incidents that reached completed/closed state. Benchmark: Good >= 95%, Watch 90-94.9%, Bad < 90%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_07: Resolution efficiency for VIP incidents. Benchmark: Good >= 95%, Watch 90-94.9%, Bad < 90%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_08: Recurrence pressure indicator tied to loyalty/retention risk. Benchmark: Good <= 15%, Watch 15-25%, Bad > 25%.
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_09: Total number of incidents in the selected period. Benchmark: Good <= 800, Watch 801-1200, Bad > 1200 (thresholds should be tuned by property scale).
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_10: Concentration of incident volume in top 5 categories; higher can indicate systemic concentration risk. Benchmark: Good <= 45%, Watch 45-60%, Bad > 60%.

corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_10: Top 5 Incident Categories Cases / Total Cases * 100

chart_notes_im.him02: Drilldown: VIP / Non-VIP → Top 10 Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him03: Drilldown: Department → Incident Category → Incident Item Name
chart_notes_im.him04: Drilldown: Incident Status → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him06: Drilldown: Severity → Incident Status → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him07: Drilldown: Department → Incident Category → Incident Item Name
chart_notes_im.him08: Stacked comparison of status mix across top incident categories.
chart_notes_im.him10: Drilldown: VIP Code → Incident Status → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him11: Drilldown: Created Date → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him15: Drilldown: Day of Week → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him17: Drilldown: Incident Category → Incident Status → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him18: Drilldown: Incident Category → Incident Item Name → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him19: Drilldown: Incident Item Name → Incident Location → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him20: Drilldown: Incident Category → Severity → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him21: Drilldown: Room No → Incident Item Name → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him23: Drilldown: Incident Category → Incident Item Name
chart_notes_im.him24: Drilldown: Severity → Incident Category → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him25: Drilldown: Incident Status → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him26: Drilldown: Source of Complaint → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him27: Drilldown: Incident Status → Aging Bucket → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him28: Drilldown: Incident Hour → Department → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him29: Drilldown: Incident Status → SLA Breach Flag → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him30: Drilldown: Arrival / Stay / Departure Stage → Incident Category → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him31: Drilldown: Room No → Incident Item Name → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him32: Drilldown: Department → SLA Breach Flag → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him33: Drilldown: Source of Complaint → Severity → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him34: Drilldown: Department → Incident Category → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him35: Drilldown: Investigation Updated By 1 → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him36: Drilldown: VIP Code → Incident Item Name → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him37: Drilldown: Booking Source → Severity → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him38: Drilldown: Company Name → Incident Category → Incident Case
chart_notes_im.him39: Drilldown: Incident Hour → Department → Incident Case

chart_formulas_im.him02: Level 1: COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY vip_type; Level 2: TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_item_name within selected vip_type
chart_formulas_im.him03: FLOW weight = COUNT(incident_case) mapped Department -> Incident Item (root-cause proxy)
chart_formulas_im.him04: COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_status; Drilldown: COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department within selected status
chart_formulas_im.him13: SUM(severity_weight) by day where Critical=4, High=3, Medium=2, Low=1
chart_formulas_im.him17: (Completed Cases in Category / Total Cases in Category) * 100
chart_formulas_im.him18: Level 1: TOP categories by COUNT(incident_case); Level 2: TOP 10 incident items by selected category
chart_formulas_im.him19: Level 1: TOP 15 incident items by COUNT(incident_case); Level 2: COUNT by incident_location within selected item
chart_formulas_im.him20: Level 1: COUNT by incident_category; Level 2: COUNT by severity within selected category
chart_formulas_im.him21: Level 1: TOP 10 room_no by COUNT(incident_case); Level 2: TOP 10 incident items within selected room
chart_formulas_im.him22: Level 1: COUNT by VIP/Non-VIP; Level 2: TOP 10 incident items within selected VIP type
chart_formulas_im.him23: Heatmap proxy from incident item frequencies split by VIP/Non-VIP segments
chart_formulas_im.him24: Level 1: COUNT by incident_category; Level 2: TOP 10 incident items within selected category
chart_formulas_im.him25: Level 1: COUNT by severity; Level 2: TOP categories within selected severity
chart_formulas_im.him26: Level 1: COUNT by incident_status; Level 2: TOP departments within selected status
chart_formulas_im.him27: Level 1: COUNT by source_of_complaint; Level 2: TOP departments within selected source
chart_formulas_im.him28: COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_status (aging-bucket proxy view)
chart_formulas_im.him30: Open Breach Proxy = Pending; Closed Breach Proxy = Total - Pending
chart_formulas_im.him31: Stage proxy split: Arrival/Stay/Departure = 20%/60%/20% of total (configurable)
chart_formulas_im.him32: Packed bubbles from TOP room_no by COUNT(incident_case)
chart_formulas_im.him33: Risk proxy = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department
chart_formulas_im.him34: Risk proxy = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY source_of_complaint
chart_formulas_im.him35: Treemap value = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department
chart_formulas_im.him37: Bubble size/value from COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY booking_source
chart_formulas_im.him38: Complaint proxy = COUNT(incident_case) by top source/company-like fields
chart_formulas_im.him39: Shift windows mapped across time ranges for handover risk visualization

chart_titles_jo.cjo03: Closing Rate vs Jobs by week

chart_titles_jo.cjo06: JO Closing Rate vs Jobs Trend by week

chart_titles_jo.cjo07: SLA Compliance vs Jobs Trend by week

chart_titles_jo.cjo18: SLA Breach Minutes by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)

chart_titles_jo.cjo19: Escalation by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)
chart_titles_jo.hjo22: Resolution P90 by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)
chart_titles_jo.cjo22: Resolution P90 by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)

chart_notes_jo.hjo01: Animated cumulative weekly donut race showing long-run share shifts by service category. Impact: sustained cumulative dominance reveals structural demand pressure points. Resolution: rebalance capacity plans, inventory, and preventive actions toward categories with persistent cumulative growth.
chart_notes_jo.cjo01: Animated cumulative weekly donut race showing long-run share shifts by service category. Impact: sustained cumulative dominance reveals structural demand pressure points. Resolution: rebalance capacity plans, inventory, and preventive actions toward categories with persistent cumulative growth.
chart_notes_jo.hjo02: Week-ascending workload bars with SLA compliance line.
chart_notes_jo.cjo02: Week-ascending workload bars with SLA compliance line.
chart_notes_jo.hjo03: Week-ascending workload bars with closing rate line.
chart_notes_jo.cjo03: Week-ascending workload bars with closing rate line.
chart_notes_jo.hjo04: Click a status slice to drill down into service category mix.
chart_notes_jo.cjo04: Click a status slice to drill down into service category mix.
chart_notes_jo.hjo05: Click a service category slice to drill down into top service items by volume.
chart_notes_jo.cjo05: Click a service category slice to drill down into top service items by volume.
chart_notes_jo.hjo06: Weekly workload (bars) versus closure efficiency (line) in ascending week order. Impact: rising jobs with falling close rate indicates backlog risk. Resolution: add short-term capacity, prioritize aging jobs, and enforce daily closure targets until rate stabilizes.
chart_notes_jo.cjo06: Weekly workload (bars) versus closure efficiency (line) in ascending week order. Impact: rising jobs with falling close rate indicates backlog risk. Resolution: add short-term capacity, prioritize aging jobs, and enforce daily closure targets until rate stabilizes.
chart_notes_jo.hjo07: Compares weekly incoming volume with SLA performance in ascending week order. Impact: SLA dips during high-volume weeks reveal process bottlenecks. Resolution: deploy surge playbook, tighten handoff SLAs, and monitor breach-prone queues hourly.
chart_notes_jo.cjo07: Compares weekly incoming volume with SLA performance in ascending week order. Impact: SLA dips during high-volume weeks reveal process bottlenecks. Resolution: deploy surge playbook, tighten handoff SLAs, and monitor breach-prone queues hourly.
chart_notes_jo.hjo08: Weekly timeout volume trend to detect service interruptions early. Impact: timeout spikes reduce guest satisfaction and increase repeat contacts. Resolution: identify root-cause weeks, fix routing/escalation delays, and set alert thresholds for timeout spikes.
chart_notes_jo.cjo08: Weekly timeout volume trend to detect service interruptions early. Impact: timeout spikes reduce guest satisfaction and increase repeat contacts. Resolution: identify root-cause weeks, fix routing/escalation delays, and set alert thresholds for timeout spikes.
chart_notes_jo.hjo09: Vertical stacked view of status mix across the top 10 departments by volume. Impact: high open/pending share in specific departments signals queue congestion. Resolution: redistribute tickets, clear blockers, and set department-level WIP limits with daily review.
chart_notes_jo.cjo09: Vertical stacked view of status mix across the top 10 departments by volume. Impact: high open/pending share in specific departments signals queue congestion. Resolution: redistribute tickets, clear blockers, and set department-level WIP limits with daily review.
chart_notes_jo.hjo10: Shows demand (bars) and close rate (line) by top categories. Impact: high-volume/low-close categories are critical performance gaps. Resolution: assign category owners, create playbooks, and track close-rate recovery by category weekly.
chart_notes_jo.cjo10: Shows demand (bars) and close rate (line) by top categories. Impact: high-volume/low-close categories are critical performance gaps. Resolution: assign category owners, create playbooks, and track close-rate recovery by category weekly.
chart_notes_jo.hjo11: Ranks the most requested service items. Impact: item concentration can create recurring operational strain. Resolution: bundle common tasks, automate repetitive steps, and pre-allocate resources for top items.
chart_notes_jo.cjo11: Ranks the most requested service items. Impact: item concentration can create recurring operational strain. Resolution: bundle common tasks, automate repetitive steps, and pre-allocate resources for top items.
chart_notes_jo.hjo12: Shows departments receiving the highest assignment load. Impact: uneven load can cause response delays and burnout. Resolution: rebalance dispatch rules and cross-train teams to absorb peaks.
chart_notes_jo.cjo12: Shows departments receiving the highest assignment load. Impact: uneven load can cause response delays and burnout. Resolution: rebalance dispatch rules and cross-train teams to absorb peaks.
chart_notes_jo.hjo13: Shows request-origin departments generating the most JOs. Impact: large demand sources may indicate upstream process gaps. Resolution: run preventive actions with source departments to reduce avoidable requests.
chart_notes_jo.cjo13: Shows request-origin departments generating the most JOs. Impact: large demand sources may indicate upstream process gaps. Resolution: run preventive actions with source departments to reduce avoidable requests.
chart_notes_jo.hjo14: Shows departments completing the highest JO volume. Impact: low completion share versus assignment share may indicate execution bottlenecks. Resolution: compare assigned vs completed mix and remove completion blockers.
chart_notes_jo.cjo14: Shows departments completing the highest JO volume. Impact: low completion share versus assignment share may indicate execution bottlenecks. Resolution: compare assigned vs completed mix and remove completion blockers.
chart_notes_jo.hjo15: Highlights locations with the largest JO demand. Impact: hotspots can degrade on-site service quality if unmanaged. Resolution: deploy location-specific staffing, stock, and preventive maintenance actions.
chart_notes_jo.cjo15: Highlights locations with the largest JO demand. Impact: hotspots can degrade on-site service quality if unmanaged. Resolution: deploy location-specific staffing, stock, and preventive maintenance actions.
chart_notes_jo.hjo16: Escalation concentration by department to identify process stress points.
chart_notes_jo.cjo16: Escalation concentration by department to identify process stress points.
chart_notes_jo.hjo17: Reassignment concentration by department to identify routing inefficiency.
chart_notes_jo.cjo17: Reassignment concentration by department to identify routing inefficiency.
chart_notes_jo.hjo18: Total breach minutes concentration by category with item drilldown. Impact: concentrated breach minutes identify where SLA risk is financially and reputationally highest. Resolution: prioritize chronic breach items for process redesign and escalation governance.
chart_notes_jo.cjo18: Total breach minutes concentration by category with item drilldown. Impact: concentrated breach minutes identify where SLA risk is financially and reputationally highest. Resolution: prioritize chronic breach items for process redesign and escalation governance.
chart_notes_jo.hjo19: Escalation concentration by category; click for item-level problem areas. Impact: high escalation indicates service instability or unclear ownership. Resolution: strengthen first-line decision rights, update runbooks, and clarify escalation triggers.
chart_notes_jo.cjo19: Escalation concentration by category; click for item-level problem areas. Impact: high escalation indicates service instability or unclear ownership. Resolution: strengthen first-line decision rights, update runbooks, and clarify escalation triggers.
chart_notes_jo.hjo20: Departments with the highest reassignment volume. Impact: frequent reassignment adds cycle time and accountability gaps. Resolution: improve assignment accuracy rules, skill mapping, and triage quality at intake.
chart_notes_jo.cjo20: Departments with the highest reassignment volume. Impact: frequent reassignment adds cycle time and accountability gaps. Resolution: improve assignment accuracy rules, skill mapping, and triage quality at intake.
chart_notes_jo.hjo21: Resolution duration profile by department for identifying long-tail execution delays.
chart_notes_jo.cjo21: Resolution duration profile by department for identifying long-tail execution delays.
chart_notes_jo.hjo22: P90 resolution time shows worst-case completion behavior by category and item. Impact: tail resolution delays drive complaints and SLA penalties. Resolution: target root-cause items with dedicated recovery plans and stricter completion SLAs.
chart_notes_jo.cjo22: P90 resolution time shows worst-case completion behavior by category and item. Impact: tail resolution delays drive complaints and SLA penalties. Resolution: target root-cause items with dedicated recovery plans and stricter completion SLAs.

imc_chart_map.cim02: Total Incident vs Status by Hotel
imc_chart_map.cim03: VIP Closure Rate vs VIP Incident by Hotel
imc_chart_map.cim05: Chain — Repeat Incident Rate by Hotel

imc_chart_notes.cim01: Shows each hotel total then top 10 incident items for drilldown prioritization. Benchmark: Good when top 3 items <= 45% of hotel incidents; Bad when top 3 items > 60% (concentration risk).
imc_chart_notes.cim02: Compares hotel volume and status mix to detect closure imbalance. Benchmark: Good when Completed >= 95% and Pending <= 5%; Bad when Pending > 10%.
imc_chart_notes.cim03: Dual-axis chart for premium guest risk and recovery effectiveness. Benchmark: Good VIP Closure >= 95%; Bad < 90%.
imc_chart_notes.cim04: Drilldown from hotel totals to top 10 categories for root-cause governance. Benchmark: Good when top category <= 20%; Bad when top category > 35%.
imc_chart_notes.cim05: Shows recurrence pressure by hotel to flag unresolved systemic issues. Benchmark: Good <= 15%; Watch 15–25%; Bad > 25%.
imc_chart_notes.cim06: Country-level map with hotel-level labels for cross-region executive visibility. Benchmark: Good when no single country exceeds 50% of chain incidents; Bad when one country > 70%.
imc_chart_notes.cim07: Hotel-to-department drilldown for operational ownership alignment. Benchmark: Good when no department exceeds 25% of hotel incidents; Bad > 40%.
imc_chart_notes.cim08: Hotel-to-source drilldown for channel quality control. Benchmark: Good when Unknown Source <= 5%; Bad > 15%.
imc_chart_notes.cim09: Stacked comparison of VIP and non-VIP load by hotel. Benchmark: Good VIP Share <= 6%; Watch 6–10%; Bad > 10%.
imc_chart_notes.cim10: Drilldown from hotel totals to booking source composition for commercial insights. Benchmark: Good when Unknown booking <= 5%; Bad > 15%.
imc_chart_notes.cim11: Executive matrix comparing risk, critical, VIP, SLA, and trend in one panel. Benchmark: Good risk score <= 60; Watch 60–100; Bad > 100.
imc_chart_notes.cim12: Ranks hotels by weighted risk for intervention priority. Benchmark: Good when high-risk hotel count reduces period-over-period; Bad when top hotel risk grows >10% WoW.
imc_chart_notes.cim13: Bubble quadrant for strategic risk classification (high volume + high severity = immediate focus). Benchmark: Good when no hotel in top-right high-risk quadrant; Bad when multiple hotels cluster there.
imc_chart_notes.cim14: Region matrix compares critical, VIP, SLA breach and trend intensity. Benchmark: Good when all risk cells trend down or stay green; Bad when >=2 metrics red in same region.
imc_chart_notes.cim15: Shows department risk intensity by hotel to target governance actions. Benchmark: Good when top department risk <= 20% of hotel total; Bad > 35%.
imc_chart_notes.cim16: Ranks root causes and cumulative contribution for improvement prioritization. Benchmark: Good when top 5 causes <= 45%; Bad when top 5 > 60%.
imc_chart_notes.cim17: Highlights unresolved critical burden (aging proxy) by hotel for escalation governance. Benchmark: Good = 0 open critical aging; Bad when persistent > 3 cases.
imc_chart_notes.cim18: Cross-hotel department matrix for fast benchmarking and imbalance detection. Benchmark: Good when cross-hotel variance is balanced; Bad when one department dominates across multiple hotels.

imc_chart_formulas.cim01: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_item_name per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim03: VIP Incidents = COUNT(vip_code valid) GROUP BY hotel; VIP Closure % = VIP Completed / VIP Incidents * 100
imc_chart_formulas.cim04: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_category per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim05: Repeat Rate % = repeat_count / total_cases * 100 per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim06: Country Value = SUM(total_cases) GROUP BY country_code; Label = CONCAT(hotel_code, incident_count) list per country
imc_chart_formulas.cim07: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim08: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY source_of_complaint per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim09: VIP = COUNT(vip_code valid); Non-VIP = total_cases - VIP; GROUP BY hotel_code
imc_chart_formulas.cim10: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY booking_source per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim13: X=COUNT(cases), Y=AVG(severity score), Bubble=VIP cases, Color=country/region
imc_chart_formulas.cim14: Regional KPI = AVG(metric by hotel in region); Regional Risk = aggregate of weighted KPI intensities
imc_chart_formulas.cim15: Department Risk Proxy = COUNT(cases) by hotel_code + department (or weighted severity where available)
imc_chart_formulas.cim17: Open Critical Aging Proxy = MIN(critical_cases, pending_cases) by hotel (aging date fallback when explicit age not present)
imc_chart_formulas.cim18: Matrix Cell = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code, department
